fields.description:
## Why
The 744b editor currently renders only structural topology with a naive layout and no property editing — it is a broken viewer, not a view+edit UX. Joe's hands-on validation surfaced the gaps below. Almost all are on OUR side (DI generation + not opting into bpmn.io capabilities), NOT bpmn.io limitations.

## What
1. **Real layered layout.** `_emit_di` sets y from nesting-lane only, so all top-level siblings share y=80 (commits & graph land at the exact same (340,80) and overlap). Generate a proper layered layout (rank=x, sibling order=y) or delegate to bpmn.io's `bpmn-auto-layout`.
2. **Edge waypoints.** We emit 2 waypoints at shape CENTERS; bpmn-js trusts imported waypoints (only re-crops on drag), so arrows cross through node text. Emit cropped/docked waypoints, or omit edge DI and auto-layout.
3. **Inline nesting.** All child shapes are placed on the single root plane with no `isExpanded` + no parent containment, so loop/map/branch bodies open in a separate drill-down canvas and children overlap. Emit EXPANDED sub-processes (isExpanded=true + child bounds inside parent) so nesting shows on one canvas (or make drill-down a deliberate, documented UX).
4. **Properties panel.** The bare Modeler has no way to view/edit a step's action (`uses`/`prompt`/`with`/`mode`/`model`/loop bounds/branch `when`) or to author a NEW step's contents — config lives in invisible `<rebar:Config>`. Wire bpmn.io's `bpmn-js-properties-panel` + a custom provider mapping rebar fields to form inputs.
5. **Branch legibility.** Branch -> gateway + opaque collapsed then/else arms hides the condition + arm contents (and is the same encoding that breaks Save — see linked bug). Reconsider the projection so the conditional is readable.

## Scope
Editor host (`editor.py build_host_html`) + DI generation (`bpmn.py _emit_di`/`_emit_step`). Out of scope: the IR semantics. Depends on the arm-id bug fix.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No two nodes overlap on open; the top frame reads left-to-right with parallel siblings on distinct rows.
- [ ] Arrows dock to node edges on open (no center-through-text routing).
- [ ] loop/map/branch bodies are visible without leaving the canvas (expanded sub-processes), or drill-down is intentional + documented.
- [ ] A user can select any step and view/edit its config via a properties panel; a newly-added step can be given its `uses`/`prompt` + `with` and Saves to valid IR.
- [ ] Branch condition (`when`) is visible/editable.
- [ ] roundtrip-demo.yaml opens legibly and demonstrates all of the above.
